title: The geographic starting point of the chronicle
summary: Jincheng, Shanxi establishes the birthplace. The chapter is intentionally brief — it sets a tone of precision before the academic acceleration begins.
whyDeep: Jincheng is the geographic baseline. Starting here signals that the biography is precise and source-honest from the first entry.

title: The first high-acceleration academic anchor
summary: Entering USTC in 1980 at age 16 for a Physics major is the first high-velocity milestone. It establishes the intellectual momentum that carries through the doctoral years and beyond.
whyDeep: USTC entry at 16 for Physics is the causal link to the scientific depth seen throughout the rest of the record. The sourcing gap here is noted — institutional confirmation would sharpen this chapter.

title: Treat the doctorate as the first stable anchor, not a decorative credential
summary: This chapter explains why Bloombase later reads like a long technical build rather than a branding-first founder story. The doctorate is the strongest pre-company anchor — the first place where the reader feels solid ground under the biography.
whyDeep: The technical depth of the Ph.D. makes the later persistence in encryption, trust infrastructure, and enterprise security legible. The sourcing is connective rather than directly institutional, so the chapter stays thesis-led.

title: The hinge between research depth and company formation
summary: This chapter keeps the biography from jumping abruptly from Hong Kong academia to Silicon Valley founder mythology. It establishes a U.S. research context before the company narrative takes over.
whyDeep: Irvine changes geography, institutions, and plausibility all at once. Even with a thin institutional record, it is the chapter that makes the transition from scientist to operator coherent.

title: Anchor the founder story in dated releases, then show how the company thickens around them
summary: The founding section is strongest when it starts with the 2013 OpenStack and VMware release trail and then layers in the 2014 advisory-board signal. That sequence makes Bloombase feel like an emerging institution in a hard trust market.
whyDeep: This is where the chronicle stops being inferred biography and becomes documented market-facing record: named role, dated publications, infrastructure-market language, then a broader company-building signal a year later.

title: Read the middle years as an accumulation of trust surfaces, not a logo parade
summary: This chapter is the durability proof. Certification, alliance status, HSM interoperability, security validation, storage-networking proof, and cloud-marketplace distribution all accumulate across different years and different parts of the stack.
whyDeep: The important pattern is cadence: Bloombase keeps reappearing where assurance matters across changing infrastructure regimes. Each artifact marks a different kind of institutional trust — certification, HSM, infrastructure, cloud distribution — so readers see progression, not repetition.

eyebrow: Deep brief 07 · Geography with a reason
title: Use geography only where place changes the strategic reading of the story
summary: The Vancouver chapter works because it is not a decorative map pin. The Launch Academy profile explains why Canada mattered strategically, while the company offices page confirms the move belongs to a real operating footprint.
whyDeep: The geography section answers one question: which location changes actually altered the company arc? Strategic rationale is paired with operating-footprint proof so each map point carries evidential weight.

title: The parallel academic arc: Founding Dean of AI and Institute co-founder
summary: From 2018 to 2023, Sean Xiang held senior academic and institutional leadership roles in Shenzhen — Founding Dean of the School of Artificial Intelligence at Shenzhen Technology University, then Chair Professor and Co-founder at the X-Institute of Shenzhen — all while leading Bloombase.
whyDeep: This chapter shows that the AI-era thesis driving Bloombase in 2023–2025 was not a pivot — it was already being built institutionally in Shenzhen from 2018 onward. The academic roles and the company arc are parallel, not sequential.

title: Close on AI infrastructure with evidence of reframing, visibility, and alliance execution
summary: The last chapter reopens the story in the present tense. The 2023 NVIDIA argument reframes the technical thesis, the public news surface shows the company still speaking, and the 2025 Utimaco announcement names the AI-era alliance directly.
whyDeep: The final section reads as a current infrastructure thesis with dated, auditable signals — not a generic claim of continued activity. Direct sourcing is strong enough here to support richer evidence treatment.
